item.title: New AirSnitch attack breaks Wi-Fi encryption in homes, offices, and enterprises - Ars Technica
item.description:
1. New AirSnitch attack breaks Wi-Fi encryption in homes, offices, and enterprises  Ars Technica
  2. Researchers discover massive Wi-Fi vulnerability affecting multiple access points — AirSnitch lets attackers on the same network intercept data and launch machine-in-the-middle attacks  Tom's Hardware
  3. Wi-Fi Security Flaws Exposed  Manufacturing Business Technology
  4. UCR computer scientists reveal Wi-Fi security flaws  University of California, Riverside

item.title: NVIDIA “unlaunches” its GeForce 595.59 driver after reports of fan and clock issues - VideoCardz.com
item.description:

  5. NVIDIA’s New GPU Driver is a Disaster & It Has Now Been Pulled Back; Did We Just See the First ‘Vibe-Coded’ Release?  Wccftech

item.title: The MOST Effective Thermal Mass Works Like A Sunburn - Hackaday
item.description:
1. The MOST Effective Thermal Mass Works Like A Sunburn  Hackaday
  2. ‘Rechargable sun battery’ outperforms lithium-ion batteries  futurity.org
  3. 'Solar battery' stores sunlight for days, then releases hydrogen on demand  Tech Xplore
  4. New Liquid Inspired by DNA-Damaging Sunburns Bottles Solar Heat for Over a Year to Warm Your House in Winter  ZME Science
  5. Scientists create liquid capable of storing solar energy for months and releasing heat afterward without batteries, wires, or outlets.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás

item.title: New Xbox CEO Asha Sharma doubles down on 'no AI slop' promise: "We won't have careless output, we won't have derivative work" - PCGamesN
item.description:
1. New Xbox CEO Asha Sharma doubles down on 'no AI slop' promise: "We won't have careless output, we won't have derivative work"  PCGamesN
  2. Xbox is in danger. Will Microsoft fix it or kill it?  The Verge
  3. What an Xbox founder thinks of the new Xbox CEO | Seamus Blackley interview  GamesBeat
  4. New Microsoft Gaming, Xbox CEO: Asha Sharma on Great Games, AI Use  Variety
  5. Xbox CEO Asha Sharma — "This team has brought it back before, and I'm here to help us do it again."  Windows Central
